scope.summary: Fix cloud hosted attachment sends that uploaded the blob and rendered a local outgoing bubble but did not deliver a Zalo attachment message to the recipient.
goals: After uploading an image via zca-rust, call Zalo's photo_original/send endpoint so the recipient receives the attachment
Persist an outgoing cloud file message only after Zalo accepts the actual attachment-send request
Attempt generic/video delivery through Zalo asyncfile/msg after upload and fail the request if Zalo rejects the send
Keep encrypted object-store handling unchanged: decrypt only in backend memory and never log plaintext file content
nonGoals: Guaranteeing all generic/video file types work before live Zalo evidence confirms asyncfile/msg accepts the uploaded metadata
Changing cloud storage encryption or object-store schemas
Adding retry queues for failed attachment sends

acceptance.description: Hosted cloud image sends call Zalo's attachment message endpoint after upload and return the Zalo msgId instead of a synthetic local id.

verification.manual: Run backend tests plus live cloud image-send smoke; inspect server/Tauri logs for successful send and no plaintext file leakage.

acceptance.description: Hosted generic/video file uploads call asyncfile/msg after upload and no longer render as successfully sent unless Zalo accepts the send request.

verification.manual: Run backend tests covering unsupported uploaded file responses and verify the API returns a bad_request rather than persisting a fake message.
